name: Master Flank Steak Beef & Citrus Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 marinated, thin flank steak (about 3/4 pound), grilled and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the lettuce, dry it, and tear into large (1- to 2-inch square) pieces. Refrigerate until ready to use.
2. Cut the flank steak against the grain into very thin slices no more than 1/4 inch thick. Refrigerate until ready to use.
3. Peel the oranges and grapefruit, cut them in half through the middle, then slice into 1/4-inch thick pieces.
4. Peel the mango and cut it into 1-inch pieces. Set aside.
5. Combine the lime juice, barbecue sauce, and vinegar in a bowl. Whisk in the peanut oil to taste (preferably less for a more pungent dressing).
6. Break the rice stick noodles into pieces about 4 to 6 inches long and place them in a large bowl of cold water. Let soften for about 15 minutes, then drain well.
7. To assemble: Toss the noodles with about 2 tablespoons of dressing and separate to thinly cover the bottom of 4 large plates. Toss the romaine with enough dressing to coat it, then place on top of the noodles. Dress the sliced flank steak with enough dressing to generously coat; arrange over the lettuce. Top with (undressed) citrus pieces and mango, and sprinkle with torn mint leaves.
8. Serves 4.
Per Serving: 475 calories, 18 g protein, 25 g carbohydrate, 37 g fat (8 g saturated), 42 mg cholesterol, 127 mg sodium, 5 g fiber.
